findings.problem: Strict implementation verification cannot run the scoped backend and frontend tests together from the repo root because both test trees resolve as a top-level Python package named `tests`, causing collection-time import collisions.
findings.evidence: Repo-root validation command `pytest -q backend/tests/test_flow_ipc_codec.py backend/tests/test_ipc_contract_guard.py backend/tests/test_ipc_v2_server.py backend/tests/test_ipc_client.py frontend/tests/hud/test_ipc_client_plugin.py` fails during collection with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tests.hud'`. Both package markers exist at `backend/tests/__init__.py:1` and `frontend/tests/__init__.py`. Split validation succeeds: backend scoped tests passed (`23 passed`), and frontend scoped plugin tests passed with `PYTHONPATH=frontend` (`15 passed`).
findings.recommendation: Remove the package-name collision so strict verification can run in one deterministic repo-root invocation. Typical fixes are renaming one test package, converting one side to non-package tests, or adjusting project pytest/pythonpath configuration so backend and frontend tests are imported under distinct module roots.
